# Agent Bridge

> Agent Bridge is a universal converter for AI agent knowledge with bidirectional sync. It transforms `.agent/` format to multiple IDE formats (Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Kiro CLI, OpenCode, Windsurf) and captures changes back, enabling true two-way sync. Supports knowledge vault management, MCP integration, snapshot management, and interactive TUI setup.

## Core Documentation

- [README](README.md): Installation, quick start, and command reference
- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): Architecture, design decisions, and contribution guide for AI agents

## Architecture

- **CLI**: `src/agent_bridge/cli.py` — argparse-based command-line interface
- **Converters**: `src/agent_bridge/converters/` — IDE format converters (cursor, copilot, kiro, opencode, windsurf)
- **Core**: `src/agent_bridge/core/` — Types, agent registry, converter base, frontmatter parser, plugin system
- **Vault**: `src/agent_bridge/vault/` — Knowledge vault manager, sources, merger
- **Services**: `src/agent_bridge/services/` — Init, sync, capture, snapshot, and status services
- **Utils**: `src/agent_bridge/utils/` — Colors, display, filesystem, MCP helpers

## Supported IDEs

| IDE | Output Directory | Capture Support |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Cursor AI | `.cursor/` | Yes |
| Kiro CLI | `.kiro/` | Yes |
| GitHub Copilot | `.github/` | Yes |
| OpenCode | `.opencode/` | No |
| Windsurf | `.windsurf/` | No |

## Quick Start

```bash
# Interactive setup
agent-bridge init

# Target specific IDE
agent-bridge init --cursor
agent-bridge init --kiro
agent-bridge init --all

# Sync vaults and refresh IDE configs
agent-bridge update

# Capture changes from IDE back to .agent/
agent-bridge capture --cursor --strategy ide_wins

# Save snapshot before major changes
agent-bridge snapshot save "stable-v1"

# Restore from snapshot
agent-bridge snapshot restore "stable-v1"

# Install MCP configuration
agent-bridge mcp --all

# Check project status
agent-bridge status
```

## Key Design Patterns

- **Self-Registering Converters**: Each converter registers itself on import. Adding a new IDE requires zero changes to services or CLI.
- **Central Agent Registry**: `core/agent_registry.py` is the single source of truth for all agent roles and capabilities.
- **Strategy Pattern for Vaults**: `GitSource`, `LocalSource`, `BuiltinSource` each know how to sync themselves.
- **Bridge Meta Tracking**: `.agent/.bridge-meta.json` maps generated IDE files back to source, enabling accurate capture status detection.

## Bidirectional Workflow

```bash
# 1. Initialize IDE configs from .agent/
agent-bridge init --cursor

# 2. Edit in Cursor IDE
# ... make changes to .cursor/agents/orchestrator.md ...

# 3. Capture changes back to .agent/
agent-bridge capture --cursor

# 4. Save snapshot
agent-bridge snapshot save "before-refactor"

# 5. Push to another IDE
agent-bridge init --kiro
```

## Testing

```bash
# Run all tests
pytest tests/

# Run specific suites
pytest tests/test_capture_service.py
pytest tests/test_roundtrip.py
pytest tests/test_plugins.py

# Current status: 137/137 tests passing
```

## For AI Agents

Read `AGENTS.md` for architecture details, design decisions, converter interface specification, and contribution workflow. The codebase follows Open/Closed Principle — extend via new converter files, not by modifying existing services.

## Optional

- [Makefile](Makefile): `make setup`, `make check`, `make format` for local development
- [docs/refactor-baseline.md](docs/refactor-baseline.md): Refactor changelog with before/after metrics
- [tests/](tests/): 137 tests covering converters, roundtrip, capture, snapshot, plugins, status
